channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Netflix is about to host videos from BuzzFeed, Condé Nast, and other publishers
item.description: Starting on August 3rd, Netflix's streaming library will include video content from dozens of digital media brands including BuzzFeed, Condé Nast, Hearst Magazines, People Inc, and Tastemade. As...

item.title: Deploying Matomo Analytics - An Open-Source Google Analytics Alternative
item.description: Matomo is an open-source web analytics platform that keeps full ownership of visitor data on infrastructure you control, a privacy-first alternative to Google Analytics. This guide deploys Matomo...

item.title: Vector Strike: Semantic Search Database Defender
item.description: Have you ever wondered how vector databases like Pinecone, Milvus, Qdrant, or pgvector search through billions of high-dimensional documents in milliseconds? Under the hood, they map semantic...

item.title: OpenAI’s Chief Futurist Is Leaving the Company
item.description: Joshua Achiam spent nearly nine years at OpenAI researching AI safety and made a memorable appearance in the Musk v. Altman trial.

item.title: Aesecnryption demo site
item.description: I rebuilt aesencryption.net so text AES (128/192/256) runs fully in the browser - the key and plaintext never leave the page. The hard part is staying byte-compatible with common server-side AES...

item.title: Deploying Plausible Analytics - Self-Hosted Web Analytics Platform
item.description: Plausible Analytics is an open-source, self-hosted web analytics tool that tracks traffic without cookies or personal data collection, a privacy-first alternative to Google Analytics. This guide...

item.title: [Boost]
item.description: The Log Is the Agent AI Engineer World's Fair Coverage Ishaan Sehgal Ishaan Sehgal Ishaan Sehgal Follow for Daily Context Jun 30 The Log Is the Agent #aie #agents #ai 48 reactions 89 comments 5...

item.title: From vexctl scripts to a governed VEX platform: building vex-ui with Next.js, keyless signing, and a Trivy-consumable repo
item.description: un Trivy against almost any vendor container image and you'll get a wall of findings. Most of them don't matter, the vulnerable code path is never executed in your deployment. The problem is that...

item.title: From Hugging Face to Amazon SageMaker Studio in one click
Ethernet Was Invented in 1973 at Xerox PARC
item.description: Every office network, every data center, and a surprising amount of factory-floor automation still runs on a technology that was sketched out in a memo more than fifty years ago. Ethernet was...

item.title: Deploying Dokploy - Self-Hosted PaaS for Docker Applications on Ubuntu 24.04
item.description: Dokploy is an open-source, self-hosted PaaS, a free alternative to Heroku, Vercel, and Netlify, that deploys apps from Git, manages databases, and routes traffic with Traefik's automatic HTTPS....

item.title: Do you test your AI agents for prompt injection? (honest answers only)
item.description: I've been building security tools for AI agents and I have a genuine question for this community. When you ship an AI powered feature a chatbot, an agent, anything that takes user input and passes...

item.title: Deploying Ngrok Ingress Controller on Kubernetes
item.description: Ngrok is a distributed reverse proxy that exposes applications to the internet via a masked external domain, without touching internal networking. The Ngrok Ingress Controller brings that model to...

item.title: Meta’s new Muse Image model can pull other Instagram users into AI photos
item.description: Meta is launching the first AI image generation model made by its Superintelligence Labs division. The Muse Image model now powers the image-making tools across the Meta AI app, Instagram, and...

item.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
item.description: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

item.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
item.description: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

item.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
item.description: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

item.title: X says top accounts steal videos from other users as it announces new video tools
item.description: Nikita Bier, X's head of product, said in a post on Monday that "[m]any videos from top accounts are simply stolen from other users, sometimes 5 years after they originally went viral," while...

item.title: The ‘G-Wagen of golf carts’ could be the ideal second car
item.description: While the auto industry wrings its hands over the electric vehicle market, sweating details like aerodynamic efficiency and range anxiety, a new EV startup based in Lisbon, Portugal, is zagging in...

item.title: ABC tells the government to get out of its newsrooms
item.description: ABC is firing back at the Federal Communications Commission after the agency opened an investigation into The View's airtime of political candidates. In a letter to the FCC on Tuesday, ABC argues...

item.title: Anthropic is launching Claude Cowork on mobile and web
item.description: Starting Tuesday, Anthropic's Claude Cowork AI platform will be available on mobile and web for the first time. The expanded access is rolling out first to Max subscribers and coming to Claude...

item.title: Google announces Pixel 11 launch event in August
item.description: Google is hosting its next Made by Google launch event for Pixel hardware on August 12th in New York City, according to an invitation sent by Google to The Verge. Unusually, the event is taking...

item.title: RedWing MaaS Packages Android Bank Fraud as a Telegram Rental Service
item.description: A new Android malware operation called RedWing is being rented out on Telegram as a ready-made bank-fraud service. It lets even low-skill criminals take over a victim's phone, steal their banking...

item.title: Rogue Agent Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Hijack Google Dialogflow CX Chatbots
item.description: A critical flaw in Google's Dialogflow CX could have let an attacker with edit rights on one Code Block-enabled agent compromise other Code Block-enabled agents in the same Google Cloud project....

item.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
item.description: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

item.title: Doom developer id reportedly cut in half as part of Xbox layoffs
item.description: As part of the mass layoffs hitting Xbox, Doom developer id Software has laid off around 50 percent of its staff, according to Game Developer. One source claimed to the publication that the cuts...

item.title: Shut Those Laptops! Anthropic Puts Its Claude Cowork Agent on Your Phone
item.description: Claude Cowork now keeps working on tasks even after you close your laptop. It’s part of a larger push toward smartphone-controlled agents.

item.title: Hugging Face Models on Foundry Managed Compute
DEBULL Tooling Abuses Microsoft Device-Code Flow to Target M365 Accounts
item.description: A Microsoft 365 device code phishing campaign has been observed leveraging collaboration-themed lures to take control of victim accounts between the last week of June 2026 and into early July, per...

item.title: Discord accidentally banned over 8,000 people for posting grids and other ‘benign’ images
item.description: Discord says a bug affecting its safety system caused it to mistakenly ban more than 8,000 accounts since May. The platform's statement follows a wave of reports from users over the past week, who...
